concerns.text: Task T043 (Power Analysis Halt) is placed in Phase 2 (Foundational) but depends on data loading/generation which occurs in Phase 3 (T012/T013/T009). The task description states it executes 'immediately after data loading', yet it is scheduled before the data loading tasks (T012, T013) and the synthetic data generator (T009) in the execution order. This creates a circular dependency: T043 needs the sample count from T009/T012 to run, but T043 is listed as a prerequisite for the user stories that contain the data generation.

Task T013 (Generate Simulated FASTQ) is listed in Phase 3 (US1) but depends on T009 (Generate Synthetic VCF) which is in Phase 2. However, T009 is marked as [P] (parallel) and placed alongside other foundational tasks. If T009 is not completed before T013 starts, T013 cannot run. The current ordering implies T013 can start in parallel with T009, violating the 'Producer before Consumer' rule. T013 must explicitly depend on T009 completion.

Task T026 (Integration test for PRS validation) is marked [P] (parallel-safe) but depends on the output of T027-T031 (Implementation of LASSO, PRS, AUC, etc.) which are in the same phase. A test task cannot run in parallel with the implementation it tests. T026 must be ordered AFTER T027-T031, not parallel to them.

Task T042 (Refactor alignment script based on profile) depends on T041 (Profile pipeline). T041 is marked [P] and placed in Phase N. T042 is also marked [P] and placed in Phase N. However, T042 explicitly states 'Read data/processed/profile_report.txt' (output of T041). If T041 and T042 are run in parallel, T042 will fail to find the input file. T042 must be ordered strictly after T041.

Task T047 (Runbook Generator) depends on T013 (Generate Simulated FASTQ) and T009 (Generate Synthetic Data). T013 is in Phase 3, T009 in Phase 2. T047 is in Phase N. The dependency chain is correct, but T047 is marked [P]. Since it aggregates outputs from multiple phases (T009, T013, T014, T017, T022, T027-T031), it cannot be parallel-safe with any of its dependencies. It must be ordered after all its dependencies complete.

Task T024 is marked as DEPRECATED but still present in the list. While not a strict ordering violation, it clutters the dependency graph. It should be removed or clearly marked as 'skipped' to avoid confusion in automated dependency resolution.
